sections_cited: Section 17-A of the West Pakistan Family Courts Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P No. S-1059 of 2024 ________________________________________________________________ _____ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on CMA No. 7954/2024 2. For hearing of main case Date of hearing and order:- 30.03.2026 None present for the petitioner __________________ The petitioner, Tahir Muhammad, has filed this constitutional petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, seeking to set aside the orders dated 15.05.2024 passed by the Family Judge, Court No.16 South, in Family Suit No. 2805/2023 and 08.08.2024 passed by the Additional Sessions Judge, Court No. VII South, in Family Appeal No. 125/2024. He averred in the pleadings that he married Respondent No.1, Mst. Faiza, on 28.02.2021, and two children were born from the wedlock, who are currently in the custody of Respondent No.1. however, the respondent filed a suit for maintenance, dowry articles, and past maintenance, while the petitioner filed a suit for restitution of conjugal rights; both suits were later consolidated. He challenged the interim maintenance order passed under Section 17-A of the West Pakistan Family Courts Act, 1964. However, after filing the petition, the petitioner failed to appear or pursue the case vigilantly. On the last hearing date, he remained absent without intimation. Consequently, this court is left with no option but to dismiss this petition in default. In such circumstances this petition is dismissed in default. JUDGE Shafi
